body.question_1: How would you rate the university's communication with parents regarding your child's academic progress and welfare?
body.answer_1: Communication from the School of Communication to parents regarding student progress is moderate and fulfills the minimum requirements, but falls short of what a genuinely family-supportive institution should provide. The parent portal provides access to basic grade and attendance data, but the interface is somewhat outdated and not particularly intuitive for parents who are not technology-confident. Proactive outreach — for example, early alerts when a student's performance or attendance begins to decline — is currently limited and reactive. More frequent, personalized, and timely communication from the Academic Affairs Office would significantly strengthen the partnership between parents and the institution in supporting student success.

body.question_2: How satisfied are you with the quality of education your child is receiving at this university?
body.answer_2: The teaching quality in my Media Studies courses at the School of Communication has been commendable this semester. My lecturers consistently demonstrate deep subject matter expertise and present complex material in a clear, well-structured, and engaging manner that truly enhances comprehension. I particularly applaud the integration of real-world industry examples and hands-on exercises into every session, which makes abstract theoretical concepts genuinely applicable. The interactive teaching style — characterized by open discussions, collaborative problem-solving, and prompt feedback on assignments — has significantly accelerated my academic growth. The faculty's visible dedication and responsiveness to students makes me confident I am receiving a high-calibre education that will serve me well in my future career.

body.question_3: How confident are you in the university's ability to prepare your child for future career opportunities?
body.answer_3: Career preparation services at the School of Communication, offered through the Career Center, are reasonable and meet some student needs while falling short in others. Basic resume guidance and periodic career fairs provide a foundation, but more intensive and personalized support would greatly benefit students planning to enter a competitive and rapidly evolving job market. The internship program has room for significant growth — expanding the employer partner network, improving placement matching, and providing structured mentorship during internship periods would make it substantially more valuable. Regular industry panels and alumni engagement events would also meaningfully bridge the gap between academic preparation and professional workplace expectations.

body.question_4: How would you assess the campus safety measures and student welfare programs in place at this institution?
body.answer_4: My confidence in the School of Communication's ability to ensure inadequate levels of campus safety and student welfare has been significantly diminished by several incidents and structural shortcomings reported to me by my child. Security coverage appears insufficient in certain areas of campus, particularly during late evening hours, and lighting in several pathways is inadequate and has been reported without timely resolution. Mental health support services are critically under-resourced — my child was advised to wait over three weeks for a counseling appointment during a period of acute personal difficulty, which is simply unacceptable. I urge the Student Affairs Office to treat student welfare as a non-negotiable priority and invest accordingly, without further delay.

body.question_5: How transparent and straightforward have you found the university's financial policies, fee management, and scholarship communication?
body.answer_5: Financial management by the Finance Department at the School of Communication has been acceptable from my perspective as a parent, meeting the fundamental requirements of tuition billing and payment processing without notable excellence or failure. Fee statements are generally accurate, though occasional discrepancies have required follow-up that is more time-consuming than it should be. Communication about scholarship timelines could be significantly improved — families benefit greatly from advance notice when disbursement dates shift or eligibility criteria change, and this is currently lacking. I would welcome a dedicated parent-facing financial FAQ and communication channel to reduce the need for direct contact for routine financial queries.

body.question_6: How effective do you find the parent portal and digital communication systems provided by the university?
body.answer_6: The parent portal at the School of Communication functions at a satisfactory level that provides access to essential information but lacks the polish and reliability expected of a modern institutional digital platform. Grade and attendance data are generally accessible, though refresh rates can be slow and the interface requires familiarity before becoming navigable. The portal is not optimized for mobile devices, which is a significant limitation given that most parents primarily use smartphones for digital access. Push notification functionality exists but is inconsistently triggered, meaning important updates are sometimes missed. A targeted UX improvement initiative — focusing on mobile responsiveness, navigation clarity, and reliable notifications — would greatly increase parental engagement and satisfaction with the digital experience.

body.question_7: How satisfied are you with the academic counseling and advising support your child receives?
body.answer_7: Academic counseling and advising services at the School of Communication, coordinated by the Academic Affairs Office, have been commendable in supporting my child's academic planning and decision-making throughout the year. Assigned academic advisors are knowledgeable, approachable, and proactively schedule meetings at key decision points in the academic calendar — such as course selection, internship planning, and graduation preparation. The quality and consistency of advice my child has received has given our family confidence that their academic trajectory is being thoughtfully guided by experienced professionals who understand both the program requirements and the student's individual goals. This service is clearly a strength of the institution.

body.question_8: How well does the university address students' mental health and personal well-being needs?
body.answer_8: Student support services at the School of Communication are acceptable in their scope and delivery, covering the essential categories but lacking depth and proactivity in several important areas that affect student well-being. Counseling services exist and are functional, but appointment availability is limited and wait times are often longer than appropriate for students in need. Financial support processes are reasonably accessible but require significant paperwork and involve delays that reduce their effectiveness in urgent situations. Investing in additional counseling staff, simplifying support request processes, and proactively promoting available services — especially during high-stress periods like mid-terms — would markedly improve the student experience.

body.question_9: How would you evaluate the quality and suitability of the university's physical facilities and campus environment?
body.answer_9: The physical campus environment and facilities managed by the Facilities Management provide a exceptional learning atmosphere that I believe genuinely contributes to academic productivity and student well-being at the School of Communication. Lecture halls are spacious, well-ventilated, and equipped with modern audio-visual technology that facilitates clear and engaging presentations. Study spaces — including individual carrels, group collaboration rooms, and outdoor relaxation areas — are thoughtfully designed and well-maintained throughout the academic week. Cleaning and security standards are consistently high, and campus maintenance requests are typically resolved within a reasonable timeframe. The overall physical environment reflects a commitment to providing students with a safe, comfortable, and inspiring place to learn.

body.question_10: What improvements would you suggest to enhance the university's support for both students and their families?
body.answer_10: My overall assessment of the School of Communication as a parent is that it offers a adequate educational experience with genuine strengths that are unfortunately balanced by structural weaknesses that need sustained attention. The academic programs are broadly well-designed, and there are individual faculty members and support staff who clearly go above and beyond for students. However, system-level inconsistencies in service quality, communication, and resource provision create an uneven experience that depends too heavily on which individual staff members a student encounters. I encourage the institution to invest in building reliable systems and consistent standards that ensure every student — regardless of program or year — receives the same high-quality support and educational experience.
